heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 18 July 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 1st for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 22 consecutive days beginning 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwave.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves; typically, 1.9 heatwaves happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 28 June 2026.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 68.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (210°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 6.9°C) and outside temperature (down 5.6°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 10%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 8223 lux) and cloud levels (up 1145 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 17476 lux) and cloud levels (up 1718 m) when compared to today.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 1490m and a high of 5530m, averaging 2440m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; temperatures should feel nicely warm. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and calm and still conditions are expected. Drizzle or light rain possible, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and some sunny spells will occur. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
